perturbation.original_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees, other individuals who are under the direct control of NatWest and work full time on NatWest premises (an "Individual Consultant"), -- all of whom --------------------- shall have a need to know said Confidential Information and who have executed a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information, Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information with anyone. This confidentiality period does not apply to InterTrust Technology, which can be disclosed at any time.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the Recipient can discuss Confidential Information with "anyone", which directly conflicts with the original text limiting discussion to NatWest employees and consultants under strict conditions. The statement that the confidentiality period doesn't apply to InterTrust Technology also contradicts the earlier part of the sentence establishing a 3-year confidentiality period for all Confidential Information, including InterTrust Technology, which can be disclosed at anytime, after being said that Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information, Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information with anyone. This confidentiality period does not apply to InterTrust Technology, which can be disclosed at any time. Recipient shall not, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above. Furthermore, without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, the Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Recipient can discuss Confidential Information with "anyone", directly contradicting the contradicted text that limits discussion to specific individuals and requires prior written permission for disclosure to others. Additionally, the changed text removes the confidentiality period for InterTrust Technology, allowing its disclosure at any time, which conflicts with the contradicted text's restrictions on disclosing Confidential Information, including InterTrust Technology, to any person without prior written permission.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned will return all physical embodiments of Confidential Information in the undersigned's possession to InterTrust promptly upon request by InterTrust, and in no event later than fifteen (15) days thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned may, at their discretion, return all physical embodiments of Confidential Information in the undersigned's possession to InterTrust upon request by InterTrust, with no specific deadline.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "will return" to "may, at their discretion, return", the clause now contradicts the original obligation to return confidential information. Removing the "fifteen (15) days" deadline creates further uncertainty and undermines the enforceability of returning the information. This creates an In-Text Contradiction on whether the Recipient is obliged to return the confidential information at all, and by when.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned may, at their discretion, return all physical embodiments of Confidential Information in the undersigned's possession to InterTrust upon request by InterTrust, with no specific deadline.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a mandatory obligation ("will return") with a discretionary action ("may, at their discretion, return") and removes the deadline ("fifteen (15) days"), while the contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, now conflicts with the original obligation. The recipient is no longer clearly required to return the information, creating a contradiction within the document. The explanation states that this "creates an In-Text Contradiction on whether the Recipient is obliged to return the confidential information at all, and by when.", which fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public through no breach of any of the undersigned's obligations under this Agreement, or NatWest's or any other NatWest employee's, obligations of confidentiality to NatWest and/or to InterTrust; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust, as evidenced through written documentation; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient without any reliance on or use of any InterTrust Confidential Information, as demonstrated through written documentation; or (iv) shall have been rightfully supplied to Recipient, with no obligation of confidentiality or non-use from a third party without any breach of any obligation of confidentiality to InterTrust or NatWest (as applicable) as of the date such information is so supplied.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public through any means; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust, regardless of documentation; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient without any reliance on or use of any InterTrust Confidential Information, regardless of documentation; or (iv) shall have been supplied to Recipient, even if with an obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The modification contradicts the original exemptions. (i) originally required that public knowledge arise through no breach of the agreement, but now states "any means", nullifying the protection against breaches. (ii) and (iii) remove the requirement for written documentation, making it more difficult to prove pre-existing knowledge or independent development. (iv) now allows information received with confidentiality obligations to be disclosed, directly violating the agreement's core premise of non-disclosure. This creates a contradiction against clause 2 by allowing all confidential information to be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public through any means; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust, regardless of documentation; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient without any reliance on or use of any InterTrust Confidential Information, regardless of documentation; or (iv) shall have been supplied to Recipient, even if with an obligation of confidentiality. In addition, the Recipient shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a court order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law; provided that the undersigned -------- ---- shall provide prompt advance notice thereof to InterTrust to enable InterTrust to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent such disclosure and shall disclose no more than the minimum information required by such court order or by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the exceptions to the confidentiality restrictions in a way that directly contradicts the core confidentiality obligations. Specifically, (i) now allows public disclosure through *any* means, even breaches of the agreement; (ii) and (iii) remove documentation requirements for proving prior knowledge or independent development, making enforcement of confidentiality more difficult; and (iv) permits the disclosure of information received under confidentiality obligations, which directly clashes with the agreement's fundamental purpose of non-disclosure. The contradicted text requires adherence to confidentiality obligations, which is directly undermined by the changes allowing broader disclosure under any means and with no regard to prior confidentiality obligations. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changes directly undermine the Confidentiality. This aligns with the definition of a Structural Flaw, which can cause in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified except by written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified orally, and any such oral modifications shall supersede any prior written agreements. This agreement may be modified except by written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement can be modified orally, which directly contradicts the following sentence stating that it can only be modified by a subsequent written agreement. This creates an uncertainty on how this agreement can be modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be modified orally, and any such oral modifications shall supersede any prior written agreements. This agreement may be modified except by written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the United States of America and the Commonwealth of Virginia, USA, without reference to conflicts of laws principles. All disputes arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia, Alexandria Division Virginia, or if jurisdiction does not properly lie in such court, the Commonwealth courts in Alexandria, Virginia.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. It initially states the agreement can be modified orally, which is then immediately contradicted by the original clause stating that modifications must be in writing. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, so the contradiction occurs within the same section of the contract. The change directly clashes with the existing clause regarding modification methods, creating uncertainty and enforceability issues.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, with the prior written permission of any InterTrust employee, disclose Confidential Information to any person they wish.
perturbation.explanation: By changing this, the Recipient can now disclose confidential information with any Intertrust employee's approval. This is contradictory to what was stated and agreed to. Now any InterTrust Employee can allow the Recipient to disclose confidential information to any person they wish, as opposed to having InterTrust's Chairman to permit this.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information, Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information with anyone. This confidentiality period does not apply to InterTrust Technology, which can be disclosed at any time. Recipient shall, with the prior written permission of any InterTrust employee, disclose Confidential Information to any person they wish. Furthermore, without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, the Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that only InterTrust's Chairman or a designated officer can authorize the disclosure of confidential information. The changed text allows any InterTrust employee to grant this permission. The contradicted text contains the changed text. The change creates a clear and direct conflict regarding who is authorized to permit the disclosure of confidential information, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction. The contradiction is that the original text limits authorization to specific individuals, while the changed text expands it to any employee, directly contradicting the intent and limitations of the original clause.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for no other purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned will use Confidential Information as they see fit, and for any purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the restricted use of confidential information outlined in the original text. This statement essentially removes any restrictions on the use of Confidential Information, allowing the recipient to use it for any purpose, directly conflicting with the original obligation to use it only in fulfillment of obligations with NatWest. This contradicts the entire purpose of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned will use Confidential Information as they see fit, and for any purposes whatsoever. The undersigned understands and acknowledges that the unauthorized use of Confidential Information may cause InterTrust very substantial damage, for which damages may be impossible to measure or inadequate to compensate. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that if he or she breaches or threatens breach of any of such sections, InterTrust will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction against him or her, from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened <PAGE> CONFIDENTIAL breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement, and Recipient hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance under such circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, "The undersigned will use Confidential Information as they see fit, and for any purposes whatsoever," directly contradicts the original text, "The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for no other purposes whatsoever." and the contradicted text which also states "The undersigned will use Confidential Information as they see fit, and for any purposes whatsoever." This removal of restrictions on confidential information creates a structural flaw where the confidentiality obligations are not maintained, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradiction is strong because it essentially nullifies the confidentiality agreement, which would cause confusion and issues of unenforceability.
